verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24δ1/TMP21) is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein of the p24 family. It has a large luminal/extracellular domain and a short cytoplasmic tail. It cycles between the ER, Golgi, and plasma membrane as part of COPI/COPII vesicle machinery, and a stable surface pool has been documented on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. It functions as a cargo receptor in the secretory pathway and reaches the cell surface where its ectodomain is accessible. It is a classical single-pass TM protein with a substantial extracellular domain, qualifying as surface accessible.
